column1:
Always prioritize clinic flow if unsure what to do.

Rooming means the full rooming workflow from the beginning of rooming until the patient is ready for checkout, including nurse visits when applicable.

MAs are the primary roomers. RNs can room, but if an RN is rooming, they are not counted as an RN acting in the RN role for the staffing scenario.

Some MA/PSR cross-trained staff can room. If they shift to rooming, they are not counted as PSR coverage for that scenario.

If help is needed, the person needing help should ask directly, in person when possible, and confirm the request was heard.

OM supports bottlenecks and acts as PSR/front desk coverage when no PSR is assigned.

Lower-priority tasks should be deferred until patient flow, triage, rooming, and front desk coverage are stable.
